title: False "Conflicting init values" error when compiling for Icestorm?
body:
Xilinx accepts the following verilog file without triggering an error: [litescope.zip](https://github.com/cliffordwolf/yosys/files/340622/litescope.zip). However, when I try to compile it with yosys, I receive the following error.

```
yosys -q -p "synth_ice40 -blif litescope.blif" litescope.v
Warning: Found one of those horrible `(synopsys|synthesis) translate_off' comments.
Yosys does support them but it is recommended to use `ifdef constructs instead!
ERROR: Conflicting init values for signal \io_storage_full [0].
Makefile:2: recipe for target 'litescope.bin' failed
```

`io_storage_full` is assigned values at lines 106. In two of these cases, the value is the same constant (9'd511), and only line 106 appears to be an initial assignment. I'm unsure what the error is referring to.

Litescope is an autogenerated Verilog file; it's possible to generate syntactically invalid Verilog. However, since Xilinx doesn't complain, I'm unsure whether Xilinx is implicitly accepting an extension to Verilog, or the above file is syntactically valid and triggers a bug.
code_blocks:
yosys -q -p "synth_ice40 -blif litescope.blif" litescope.v
Warning: Found one of those horrible `(synopsys|synthesis) translate_off' comments.
Yosys does support them but it is recommended to use `ifdef constructs instead!
ERROR: Conflicting init values for signal \io_storage_full [0].
Makefile:2: recipe for target 'litescope.bin' failed
